title: Understanding the memory pattern as a function of toys

posts.name: Afiq Aizuddin Anuar
posts.text:
Hi experts,
In making the tests that led to my question in the other thread, I find an unexpected memory usage pattern that increases as the number of requested toys increase. The test is done with the command [1]. The resulting maximum memory usage is [2]. In other areas such a pattern is indicative of memory leaks, and I wonder if this may not be the case here as well.
Cheers,
Afiq
[1]
/usr/bin/time -f '%Uuser %Ssystem %Eelapsed %PCPU (%Xtext+%Ddata %Mmax)k' combineTool.py -v -1 -M MultiDimFit -d workspace_twin-g.root -m 400 -n _pnt_g1_0.0_g2_0.0_toys_0 --algo fixed --fixedPointPOIs 'g1=0.0,g2=0.0' --setParameters 'g1=0.0,g2=0.0'  --cminPreScan --cminDefaultMinimizerAlgo Migrad --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,Simplex,0 -s -1 --toysFrequentist -t 10 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_analytic

[2]
ntoy max_memory
5 660860max
10 678128max
20 952588max
40 1348592max
80 2137032max
160 3637392max
posts.cooked_html:
Hi experts,

In making the tests that led to my question in the other thread, I find an unexpected memory usage pattern that increases as the number of requested toys increase. The test is done with the command [1]. The resulting maximum memory usage is [2]. In other areas such a pattern is indicative of memory leaks, and I wonder if this may not be the case here as well.

Cheers,
Afiq

[1]

/usr/bin/time -f '%Uuser %Ssystem %Eelapsed %PCPU (%Xtext+%Ddata %Mmax)k' combineTool.py -v -1 -M MultiDimFit -d workspace_twin-g.root -m 400 -n _pnt_g1_0.0_g2_0.0_toys_0 --algo fixed --fixedPointPOIs 'g1=0.0,g2=0.0' --setParameters 'g1=0.0,g2=0.0'  --cminPreScan --cminDefaultMinimizerAlgo Migrad --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 0 --cminFallbackAlgo Minuit2,Simplex,0 -s -1 --toysFrequentist -t 10 --X-rtd MINIMIZER_analytic

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Afiq,
I think it’s been seen before. May be on the RooFit side instead of ours, but worth checking again. Can you make a github issue (https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/issues) to fully document and add links to your inputs so we can reproduce.
thanks,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Afiq,

I think it’s been seen before. May be on the RooFit side instead of ours, but worth checking again. Can you make a github issue (https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/issues) to fully document and add links to your inputs so we can reproduce.

thanks,
Andrew

posts.name: Afiq Aizuddin Anuar
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
Alright, will do.
Cheers,
Afiq
